title: quercus glauca subsp. longinux
intro: Quercus glauca subsp. longinux is a subspecies of the evergreen oak tree native to Japan. It is a type of deciduous tree known for its medicinal properties, which have been utilized in traditional herbal medicine for centuries. The bark and leaves of this plant contain a variety of bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits such as reduced cardiovascular risk, improved immune function, and anti-cancer effects. Quercus glauca subsp. longinux is often prepared as a tea or decoction, as well as being used in traditional herbal supplements and extracts.
